trade.summary: Korea-Thailand trade has grown substantially under the ASEAN-Korea FTA and RCEP. Korea exports steel, chemicals, electronics, and machinery to Thailand. Thailand exports automotive parts (from Japanese-owned factories), rubber, and electronics assemblies. Korea-Thailand trade benefits from Thailand's role as an ASEAN manufacturing hub. Samsung, LG, and Hyundai have Thai operations.

diplomatic.summary: Korea-Thailand relations are warm, with K-pop and Korean cultural influence having a major following in Thailand. Both countries cooperate through ASEAN+3 and bilateral frameworks. Korea is an important development model for Thailand. Korean Wave (Hallyu) cultural exports have significantly enhanced soft power and people-to-people ties.

military.summary: Growing defense relationship. Korea has exported defense equipment to Thailand. The Royal Thai Navy and Air Force have shown interest in Korean defense platforms. Both countries participate in multilateral ASEAN-affiliated exercises. No formal bilateral defense treaty.

financial.summary: Korean FDI in Thailand is significant and growing, with Samsung, LG, POSCO, and Hyundai Steel having major operations. Korean companies use Thailand as a Southeast Asian manufacturing base. The Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC) has attracted significant Korean investment commitments.

energy.summary: Korea imports some LNG and natural gas from Thailand. Korean energy companies (Korea Gas, SK) have invested in Thai upstream energy. Growing cooperation on renewable energy and battery storage. Limited but growing bilateral energy trade.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific cooperation in ICT, smart manufacturing, and environmental science. KOICA runs development programs in Thailand. Korean universities and KAIST have exchange programs with Thai institutions. Cooperation on digital economy and smart city technologies is expanding.
